message:
Harden sync notify baton-passing and simplify once_cell INITIALIZING state

notify.rs — baton-passing on early removal:
- When a Notified future is removed from the waiter list before consuming
  its notification (e.g., generation changed and the future completes
  early), the previous code just dropped the lock, silently losing the
  notification. Now both early-return paths in poll() check whether the
  removed entry had (notified=true, generation==initial_generation) and
  if so call pass_baton() to forward the notification to the next eligible
  waiter, preventing a lost-wakeup scenario.
- The existing Drop impl already had baton-passing logic for the
  cancellation case; that code is now replaced by a call to the same
  pass_baton() method, deduplicating the logic. pass_baton() iterates
  entries looking for an un-notified waiter with a live waker, marks it
  notified, wakes it, and returns. If no waiter is found, it increments
  stored_notifications so the next Notified::poll() picks it up.

once_cell.rs — INITIALIZING state simplification:
- Previously, set_blocking() would call wait_for_init_blocking() when
  the CAS found INITIALIZING, then loop to retry. This could block the
  thread indefinitely in async contexts where the initializer task might
  be on the same thread.
- Now immediately returns Err(value) when INITIALIZING is observed,
  treating the cell as "claimed" by another thread. The caller can retry
  at a higher level if needed, but the thread is never blocked. This is
  safe because the cell's value semantics are "first writer wins" and
  the INITIALIZING state means a winner already exists.

rwlock_lost_wakeup_test.rs — new exploratory test:
- Standalone test exercising the RwLock writer-after-reader-wake sequence
  to verify no lost wakeups when a writer acquires between reader
  notification and reader poll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
